title: Angle relationships with parallel lines
description: Find missing angles given two parallel lines and a transversal.
topic_path: Get ready for congruence, similarity, and triangle trigonometry

assessment_items.question_content:
Below are two parallel lines with a third line intersecting them.



[[☃ image 1]]

$x =$ [[☃ input-number 1]] $^\circ$
hints: Notice that the two angles are in the same position but at different intersections.
One way to describe the angles is to say that they are corresponding angles.  Corresponding angles are always equal.

Angle $x$ is $116^\circ$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines with a third line intersecting each line. Where the third line crosses the top parallel line, the top, left angle measure is x degrees. Where the third line crosses the bottom parallel line, the top, left angle measure is 116 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below are two parallel lines with a third line intersecting them.





[[☃ image 1]]

$x = $ [[☃ input-number 1]] $^\circ$
hints:
Vertical angles are equal, so the pink angle measures $122$ degrees.



[[☃ image 1]]

The pink and green angles are corresponding angles, so they are also equal.
Angle $x$ is $122^\circ$. 



[[☃ image 1]]

options.alt: Two parallel lines with a third line intersecting each line. Where the third line crosses the left most parallel line, the top, right angle measure is x degrees. Where the third line crosses the right most parallel line, the bottom, left angle measure is 122 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
Below are two parallel lines with a third line intersecting them.





[[☃ image 1]]

$x = $ [[☃ input-number 1]] $^\circ$
hints:
The pink angles are adjacent to the blue angle and form a straight line, so they measure:

 $\qquad180^\circ - 46^\circ = 134^\circ$ 


[[☃ image 1]]
The pink angles are equal because they are opposite each other.
One of the pink angles corresponds with the green angle, and the other pink angle forms an alternate interior angle.

Angle $x$ equals the pink angles and measures $134^\circ$.

[[☃ image 1]]

 Note that the blue and green angles are supplementary.

options.alt: Two parallel lines with a third line intersecting each line. Where the third line crosses the top parallel line, the bottom, right most angle measure is x degrees. Where the third line crosses the bottom parallel line, the top, right most angle measure is 46 degrees.
